contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: Wire AWS access into production autopilot + graceful Health-API degrade
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_autopilot/pull/36
  
  Details:
  Closed the production autopilot's AWS-monitoring gap end-to-end after Gary asked to make sure production has all the credentials local has.
  
  ## What shipped
  - Synced /opt/truesight_autopilot/.env on production from local (same 9 key names; values updated where they had drifted).
  - Appended Cypher AWS credentials (account 767697632458, validated minutes earlier via the EC2 dry-run diagnostic) to production .env.
  - Backed up prior production .env to /opt/truesight_autopilot/.env.backup.20260513_184334 for revert safety.
  - Restarted truesight-autopilot.service; AWSMonitor now reports 'AWS CloudWatch connected' and 'AWS daily spend (2026-05-13): $0' (Cost Explorer working).
  - Shipped truesight_autopilot PR #36: aws_monitor.py now gracefully degrades when AWS Health API returns SubscriptionRequiredException (Cypher account lacks Business support). Once-and-done INFO log instead of ERROR-spam every poll.
  - Pulled #36 onto production, restarted, verified clean journal: CloudWatch + Cost Explorer working, Health polling silently disabled, no residual errors.
  
  ## Architecture impact
  Production autopilot now actively polls CloudWatch (EC2 health metrics) and Cost Explorer (daily spend). If spend or instance health anomalies arise on the Cypher account, autopilot will surface them automatically rather than relying on Gmail-forwarded AWS alerts. AWS Health (incident/maintenance API) requires Business+ support; the graceful-degrade keeps that limitation visible at startup but invisible during normal operation.
  
  ## Sequence captured for the next session
  1. cypher_def/.env CYPHER_DEFENCE_AWS_KEY rejected → Gary refreshed → reauth succeeded.
  2. EC2 RunInstances --dry-run returned DryRunOperation → confirmed account-level restriction has been LIFTED.
  3. Production autopilot lacked AWS env vars → AWSMonitor was failing with NoCredentialsError every restart for at least 24h before the fix.
  4. After wiring + restart: SubscriptionRequiredException surfaced (account-level limitation, distinct from credential issue) → graceful-degrade fix shipped + deployed.
  
  PR: https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_autopilot/pull/36 (merged + deployed)
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
